The judge then inquires about his age and talent, and Kevin states he's 25 and does "magic," prompting another wave of audience excitement and a woman judge in a bright yellow top to exclaim, "We love magic!" The bald judge then tells Kevin he's in the right place, as "magic is magic on this show," and asks why he chose YAGT. The camera alternates between Kevin's expressive gestures, the judges' engaged reactions (including the third judge in a dark suit listening intently), and wide shots of the packed theater, maintaining a lively and energetic atmosphere. He addresses the judges, stating he has 'a few photos of us here,' naming 'Simon, Heidi, Sophia, Howie, Terry, and myself' to 'help take a mental snapshot of this moment.' He then walks toward the judges' table, handing a photo to Judge 2, the woman in the yellow top, whom he addresses as 'Heidi.' Heidi responds with 'Hello,' 'Yes,' and 'Okay' as Kevin Li instructs her not to look at the photo just yet. The audience claps and reacts throughout, with their applause audible, as the stage lighting dynamically shifts between warm orange and cool blue hues against a stylized cityscape backdrop. Kevin Li instructs Heidi to 'hide the photo, put it away' when she's done, urging everyone to 'do your best to vividly remember all the details.'

The tension builds, culminating in audience gasps and shouts of "Oh no!" and "No!", along with Kevin Li's own "Whoa!" and a woman's scream, as the electronic tone returns and crescendos. Judge 1, the bald man, reacts with open-mouthed surprise, while the vibrant blue and purple stage lighting illuminates the scene, highlighting the Sonic-branded cups and microphones on the judges' table, maintaining the show’s energetic atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: Kevin Li, clad in a beige jacket and pants over a white t-shirt, captivated the audience and judges with a dramatic performance, beginning by asking Judge 1, Simon, what was inside a box, to which Simon replied, "Nothing." Kevin then revealed a folded piece of paper labeled 'CORE MEMORIES' which he dramatically tore apart, with Judge 2 catching the pieces and exclaiming, "Wow!" as the audience cheered. Transitioning to a more emotional tone, Kevin removed his jacket to reveal a corkboard with six photos of children, stating, "We tend to focus a lot on the future, but sometimes we forget about all the memories that made us who we are today." He invited the judges to "rewind the hands of time," prompting Judge 1 to exclaim, "Whoa!" and Judge 2 to recognize, "Ah, it's us!" Kevin playfully commented on their youthful appearances, eliciting laughter. The stage was bathed in vibrant blue and purple lighting, with a cityscape backdrop and an illuminated judges' table featuring Sonic-branded cups. Later, Kevin, now in a white t-shirt and dark pants, emotionally declared, "These kids, especially me, would have never dreamed we'd all be here together," receiving cheers. Judge 2, Heidi, expressed excitement, stating, "I love it! I got goosebumps!" Kevin then handed out Polaroid-style photos: a red rose to Heidi, a person in a dark setting to Judge 3, and a photo of hands holding a small object to Heidi. Heidi praised his magic, calling his eyeball trick "disgusting, but so cool." Judge 2, Sofia, later described Kevin's performance as "spectacular" and noted his emotional transformation. Judge 1, Simon, called Kevin "good," "cool," "different," "slick," and "Vegas." Judge 3, Howie, reflected on Kevin's journey from watching the show as a child to performing on stage. The performance concluded with Kevin wiping his eye, met with empathy and laughter from the judges and continued applause from the audience on the ornately detailed stage.

Judge 2 asks Brynn her age, to which she replies, "I'm 12," and Judge 3 inquires about her origin, prompting Brynn to state, "We're from Paw Paw, Michigan," drawing further audience applause. The host is briefly seen off-stage, smiling warmly. Brynn stands center stage with the puppet on a small stand, engaging with the audience and judges as the stage backdrop shifts to a vibrant blue with dynamic light patterns. Judge 2 asks how she got into ventriloquism, and Brynn explains she was inspired by seeing Darci Lynn on AGT, declaring, "That's what I'm gonna do." Judge 2 praises her, saying, "And look at you now! Judge 3 then asks about her plans if she wins a million dollars, to which Brynn humorously replies she'd like to follow Darci's footsteps, start touring, get a tour bus, and "really want a bearded dragon," eliciting laughter and an "Oh, wow!" from Judge 3, who then encourages her, "Okay, we're ready, go ahead." The video transitions from a dramatic performance to a lighthearted and whimsical act as Brynn Cummings, a young girl with long brown hair wearing a dark green dress with white heart patterns, confidently walks onto the stage holding a pink and white furry puppet with large blue eyes and a tuft of white hair. The puppet, speaking in a high-pitched, slightly whiny voice, declares, "I don't wanna sing anymore," to which Brynn, speaking into a small microphone clipped to her dress, responds with exasperation, "What? This is what we've practiced for weeks." The puppet insists, "They wanna see something else, something new," prompting a chuckle from Judge 1 (the bald man). Brynn questions, "But we haven't practiced anything else. What's your plan?" The puppet excitedly exclaims, "I've got it! Mentalism!" Judge 1, surprised, echoes, "Mentalism?" and Brynn adds, "Wait, what?" as the audience claps and cheers. The puppet, dismissive, tells Brynn, "I don't mind, Reed. Quit playing around, let's just sing the song. Trust me!" It then announces, "Taking down to the judges." Brynn asks, "Is that allowed?" and Judges 3 (the man in the dark suit), 1, and 2 (the woman in the yellow top) all confirm with "Yeah," "Sure," and "Yeah" respectively, as Brynn walks toward the judges' table, holding the puppet. The audience erupts in cheers and applause, accompanied by light, magical background music. Judge 2, whose name is revealed to be Heidi, remarks, "She's so good," with Judge 1 agreeing, "He is good." Brynn expresses worry, "I have a bad feeling about this," but the puppet reassures her, "Oh, stop worrying." Brynn, frustrated, states, "Seriously, I don't know what I'm supposed to do." The puppet confidently declares, "It's okay, we'll use a deck of cards. Check your pocket," as the magical music continues. Brynn, confused, asks, "Wait, what?" before the puppet triumphantly announces, "Ta-da!" The audience reacts with delight. The puppet then addresses Judge 2, "Heidi, I'd like you to cut the deck one time," as Brynn stands beside the judges, the puppet perched on the table, and the audience applauds warmly, maintaining the cheerful, family-friendly atmosphere. Judge 2 reaches out to touch the puppet, and close-ups capture the puppet’s expressive face and Brynn’s animated expressions.

event_blocks.summary: On the America's Got Talent stage, Brynn Cummings, accompanied by her pink and white furry puppet, received a unanimous four "yes" votes from the judges, eliciting roaring applause and cheers from the audience and celebratory music. Host, a man in a dark suit, congratulated Brynn, who emotionally declared it the "best moment of my life" before being guided backstage. The scene then shifted to Mandy Muden, a performer from London, who confidently took her place at the Judges' table, engaging the judges with humor and charm. She playfully questioned her age, stated she "love[s] America!" and "owe[s] money," and quipped about spending a million dollars "on men." Mandy then proceeded to perform magic, first asking Sofia Vergara to draw letters from a bag, then instructing Simon Cowell to place an object in her book, which he questioned was a "finger." She then had Howie Mandel think of a word, revealing a Collins Pocket English Dictionary from a brown paper bag, impressing him. Heidi Klum revealed her "love story" word was "50 Shades of Grey," and Simon stated his page number was "70." Mandy then asked Sofia if her word on page 70 began with 'F' and ended in '-ING,' which Sofia confirmed, leading Mandy to write 'FING' on her sketchpad. After Sofia confirmed familiarity with the word, Mandy revealed it was "fantasizing," eliciting cheers. Later, Mandy instructed a judge to find the word "Looney" on page 330, seven entries down, but she corrected him, revealing her word was "Looney." She then gathered letters from other judges: Simon's word was "Eat," Howie's letter was "E," Heidi's was "A," and Sofia's was "A T," exclaiming "Unbelievable!" as triumphant music played and the audience erupted in applause. Mandy presented the blue-covered Collins Pocket English Dictionary to a judge, who remarked, "Mandy, that was quite a surprise!" The brightly lit stage featured dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns and the show's logo.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Mandy Muden, a blonde woman in a leopard-print jacket over a black dress and a large white statement necklace, confidently performing on the brightly lit America's Got Talent stage, which features dynamic blue and gold geometric patterns and a large screen displaying the show's logo and judges' names. Mandy, speaking with an excited tone, exclaims, "It's great to have so much fun!" and addresses Judge 1, "Howie!" Judge 1, a bald man in a black denim jacket, identified as Howie, reacts with laughter and amusement, praising Mandy with a calm, appreciative voice: "You're a very good magician. You're funny, I love you. I'm a huge fan of yours. You are the epitome of somebody that deserves to have their own show. I would go see just you in Las Vegas." The audience, visibly entertained, claps enthusiastically as Mandy, overwhelmed, responds, "Yes! Oh wow, thank you so much! Thank you!" She then calls out, "Heidi!" to Judge 2, a woman in a bright orange top. Heidi, with a warm and playful voice, calls Mandy "adorable" and asks if she's "gonna keep this?" to which Mandy playfully replies, "Yes, of course!" Mandy then addresses Judge 3, "Simon!" Simon, a man in a dark long-sleeve shirt, smiles broadly and, with a distinct, slightly critical but ultimately appreciative voice, comments, "We saw everything, didn't we? I mean, you were mind-reading, you were funny, you're very naughty. I really like you." Mandy gratefully replies, "Oh, thank you!" before Simon adds, "I do." As the camera alternates between close-ups of Mandy's expressive face and wide shots of the stage, Mandy announces, "Now we're gonna vote!" She prompts Heidi, who enthusiastically declares, "It's a yes from me for Mandy!" Mandy thanks her, then turns to Simon, who confirms, "It's a yes." Finally, Mandy calls on Howie, who loudly exclaims, "Three yeses!" Amidst roaring audience applause and celebratory music, Mandy joyfully shouts, "Four yeses! Oh, thank you so much! That's amazing!" The segment culminates with the entire theater, including the judges, standing and applauding enthusiastically under vibrant purple and blue stage lighting, celebrating Mandy's successful act. Each judge has a branded Sonic cup with a red straw at their illuminated table.

event_blocks.summary: Mandy Muden energetically traversed the America's Got Talent stage, waving to the audience amidst celebratory music and applause before approaching the Judges' table, expressing thanks. Judge 1, a bald man, laughed heartily and commented on her skill, while Judge 2, in an orange top, expressed shock and admiration, referencing Howie as a hero. Judge 3, in a dark shirt, was also seated at the table. Following a wide shot of the stage and audience, the focus shifted to a nervous but composed young boy named Ryland, identified by a yellow nameplate and dressed in a teal polo shirt and brown pants. Judge 2 warmly welcomed him, and after Ryland stated his name, age (10), and origin (England), eliciting cheers, the judges reacted with smiles and applause. Ryland then revealed his talent as a magician, having practiced for eight years since age two, which prompted audible 'Oohs!' and 'Wows!' from the judges. Later, Ryland, speaking with a British accent, explained he was 'born with magical powers' and received some from his magician dad, leading to audience cheers and a 'Wow!' from a male voice. When asked about winning a million dollars, Ryland stated he'd share it with his family and 'spend the rest on Legos,' drawing more cheers and a 'Wow!' from the audience and judges. Encouraged by the female judge, Ryland announced his intention to create a new magic word and revealed he had drawn pictures for each judge, handing them out to Howie, Heidi, Sofia, and Simon, instructing them not to peek. Ryland then explained his act, involving a group of five young men in black shirts holding numbered cards, instructing the judges to pick a friend and a number. Howie chose 'Mark' and 'seven,' placing it first, while Heidi selected '11' for the fourth position. Sofia was then addressed by Ryland. The performance continued with Ryland asking Simon (Judge 1) to choose between '24 or 10' and 'Two or three,' with Simon selecting '10' and 'Two.' Simon then chose 'Jordan' and 'One' from Ryland's friends, and when asked about placement, humorously exclaimed, 'He's taking my position!' as Ryland proposed a 'different' move. The act transitioned to illustrated cards depicting 'Lego, Dragons, burgers,' with Ryland highlighting the chosen cards: a snake, a lime, a house, and mountains, spelling 'SCAM.' The judges confirmed the word, and Ryland instructed the audience to shout 'SCAM!' on the count of three, which they did loudly, followed by laughter and applause. Ryland then built suspense by asking what if he knew the word beforehand, instructing the judges to reveal their drawings from envelopes. Judge 2 held up a lime card and Judge 3 a house card, matching Ryland's arrangement, causing the audience to gasp and cheer as the music swelled and Ryland triumphantly declared, 'As you can see, they match,' concluding the interactive performance on the dynamically lit stage.

The segment opens with Judge 2, a woman in a bright yellow top, smiling intently at the Judges' table. On the dynamically lit 'America's Got Talent' stage, Ryland, a young boy in a teal polo, stands confidently before a black music stand, directing a group of young men in black shirts (including 'JAKE', 'AUGUST', 'MARK', 'JORDAN', and 'JUSTIN') who hold illustrated cards. Ryland's clear voice explains, "Each of you had a free choice of which drawing you wanted, and more importantly, where to put it." He then points to the colorful hand-drawn cards—a snake, a lime, a house, and mountains—as he articulates, "The snake looks like an S, the lime looks like a C, the house looks like an A, and the mountains look like an M. That spells the word SCAM." Judge 1, the bald man, confirms with "Right?", followed by Judge 2's "Right," and Judge 3's "Yeah." Ryland continues, announcing they'll create a "brand new magic word," which elicits audience laughter and applause. He then instructs, "Everyone shout SCAM as loud as they can on the count of three. One, two, three..." The audience, illuminated by blue and purple stage lighting, erupts with a loud, unified "SCAM!", followed by more laughter and applause. Ryland then builds suspense, asking, "But what if I told you that we knew what the magic word would be before you even picked it?" He instructs the judges, "I'd like to take the drawing out of the envelope and show it to everyone," as suspenseful, upbeat music begins to play. Judge 2 holds up a lime card and Judge 3 a house card, mirroring the sequence Ryland arranged. The audience gasps and cheers as the judges reveal their matching cards, confirming the coordinated act. Ryland triumphantly declares, "As you can see, they match," as the music swells and the audience bursts into thunderous cheering and applause, fully immersed in the lively, interactive performance on the dynamic blue and gold geometric stage, complete with Sonic cups and illuminated 'X' symbols at the Judges' table.

event_blocks.summary: The America's Got Talent stage, illuminated by vibrant blue and purple lighting with dynamic geometric patterns, hosted Magic Mike Jacobson, a performer in a mustard-yellow jacket. He engaged the judges—Simon (bald man), Heidi (woman in yellow top), Sofia (woman in yellow top), and Howie (man in dark suit)—and the audience with a card magic trick. Initially, Magic Mike explained the trick involved burning an invisible deck of cards, asking judges to choose between number and face cards, and then men or women, with Sofia choosing faces and Howie opting to burn the men. He then instructed Heidi to use a match to draw the suit of a queen to save. Later, he had Howie shuffle an invisible deck and pick a card, instructing him to sign it with an invisible Sharpie. Magic Mike revealed that every card in the deck was face up except for one, which was Howie's chosen card, the Ten of Clubs, with Howie's signature on it, creating a moment of disbelief and astonishment among the judges and audience. Heidi described the audience's rapt attention and called the performance mesmerizing and fantastic, while Simon predicted Magic Mike was born to perform magic, stating no one was in on the trick. The judges unanimously gave him four "yes" votes. After leaving the stage, Magic Mike expressed feeling amazing and pumped, while backstage, judges examined Polaroid photos of the trick, with Sofia exclaiming about the craziness of the signature and the outcome.
